_meta.purpose: Illustrative scenarios for platform worked examples: how much a worker grossed and what they spent earning it. These are NOT tax figures. Every rate applied to them — the 72.5c mileage rate, the 80% gig-driver phone business-use share, SE tax, brackets — comes from the verified dataset. What lives here is only the shape of the example.
_meta.why: The platform entries in legacy/data/config.json carry incomeType, fees and a list of deduction categories, but no example income and no usage figures. A worked example needs both. Inventing a tax rate would break ground rule 2; choosing a plausible scenario to apply verified rates to does not — but it is a choice, so it is recorded here in the open rather than buried in a template.
rules: Round, obviously-illustrative numbers. These are not survey data and must never be presented as typical earnings.
Pages must describe them as an example, never as what a worker on this platform earns.
Any figure that is a tax rate, threshold or limit belongs in the verified dataset, not here.

doordash._supplies: insulated bags and phone mounts — the platform's keyDeductions list names insulated_bag
doordash._note: Delivery driving is mileage-dominated: at the 2026 business rate the mileage deduction alone is the single largest number on the page, which is the point the page makes.
doordash._ladder: Three illustrative volumes. Miles scale with gross because delivery pay is roughly per-drop; the ratio is held near the base scenario's rather than modelled.
